changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_policy_captures_event_outcomes to k8s_audit_policy_event_outcomes_captured for better clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to audit logging, which is a critical component of security in Kubernetes. The original function names mostly followed the naming conventions, but a minor change was made to improve clarity and consistency. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls related to audit logging.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly describe the check and follow the pattern k8s_<resource>_<check_description>
title: Content Of Audit Records - Outcome of the event; and
step1_reasoning: The control AU-3-e focuses on ensuring that audit records capture the outcome of events. In Kubernetes, this is directly related to configuring audit logging to capture detailed information about API server interactions, including the outcome of events. The Kubernetes audit policy can be configured to include event outcomes, and ensuring audit logging is enabled and properly retained aligns with this control.
